summary: Split the remaining large stage assembly block out of asm/text_4.s into dedicated target-stage assembly files and one shared address-named assembly file. The PR removed 4702 lines from asm/text_4.s, added 19 grt*.s files plus asm/code_80224A54.s, and updated obj_files.mk and ldscript.lcf so the new objects are built and linked in the intended order.
what_changed: Deleted a 4702-line contiguous assembly chunk from asm/text_4.s.
Added asm/code_80224A54.s with 746 lines of assembly for the shared code range beginning at func_80224A54.

Added separate target-stage assembly files for many character-specific target stages: grticeclimber, grtkirby, grtkoopa, grtlink, grtluigi, grtmars, grtmewtwo, grtness, grtpeach, grtpichu, grtpikachu, grtpurin, grtsamus, grtseak, grtyoshi, grtzelda, grtgamewatch, grtemblem, and grtganon.
Updated obj_files.mk to add the new asm objects to TEXT_O_FILES immediately after existing grtdrmario.o, grtfalco.o, and grtfox.o entries and before the remaining asm/text_4.o entry.
Updated ldscript.lcf FORCEFILES with the new grt*.o object files so the linker preserves the split-object ordering.
smart_moves: Kept this as a pure split/reorganization rather than trying to rename or decompile the functions at the same time, reducing matching risk.
Preserved original global labels and address comments in the new assembly files, making the split auditable against the old text_4.s range.
Mirrored the new object ordering in both obj_files.mk and ldscript.lcf, which is important for matching projects where object order affects final addresses.
Separated the generic/shared range into asm/code_80224A54.s instead of forcing it into a character-specific grt file when the evidence did not support a better semantic name.
decomp_lessons: When breaking up a monolithic asm file, update both the build object list and linker FORCEFILES; adding the .s files alone is not enough.
For assembly-only progress, address-based filenames such as code_80224A54.s are acceptable placeholders when semantics are still unclear.
A low-risk split should preserve existing func_ and lbl_ names until later decompilation work can assign stronger names.
Character or stage module splits are easier to search and review when each internal target-stage unit gets its own grt*.s file.
naming_conventions: Target-stage assembly files use the grt<character>.s pattern, as seen in grticeclimber.s, grtmewtwo.s, grtpurin.s, and grtganon.s.
The PR retained existing placeholder symbol conventions such as func_80224A54 and lbl_8049E6C8.
Unclassified shared assembly was named by start address as code_80224A54.s.
Internal file spellings in this slice include grtmars.s and grtseak.s; the PR provides no commentary explaining those spellings.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Each new assembly file includes macros.inc and declares .section .text, matching the style of existing asm files.
The split preserved instruction address comments and raw PowerPC instructions, indicating an object-boundary reorganization rather than source rewriting.
The object list in obj_files.mk places new grt*.o files before asm/text_4.o, consistent with moving earlier ranges out of the monolithic text file.
ldscript.lcf FORCEFILES was extended with the same grt*.o units to stabilize link order.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body was empty, so intent is inferred from the title, file list, and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or rename functions from asm/code_80224A54.s.
Find later PRs touching grticeclimber.s, grtmewtwo.s, grtpurin.s, or grtganon.s for semantic stage names.
Check whether grtmars and grtseak remain canonical filenames or were later renamed.
Search for subsequent removals from asm/text_4.s to understand the remaining monolithic assembly split pattern.
